specialists.why: To clarify the security posture of the SQLite database and logs containing potentially sensitive data.

items.text: Are the SQLite databases and system logs encrypted at rest, and what access controls are enforced on the host environment?

specialists.why: The assessment requires clarification on whether personal information is processed and where the privacy compliance documentation is stored.

items.text: Does the structured data ingested from SQL databases and CSVs contain any personal or sensitive information?
Where are the Privacy Threshold Assessment and/or Privacy Impact Assessment for this project stored?

specialists.why: To resolve ambiguities regarding data sensitivity, stakeholder engagement, and team composition for the Tripwire pipeline.

items.text: Does the structured data ingested from CSVs and SQL databases contain any personally identifiable information (PII) or demographic attributes that could trigger algorithmic bias?
Have the identified stakeholders, specifically the IT Security Advisors and business owners, been consulted regarding the pipeline's fail-closed design and operational thresholds?
Are there plans to include individuals with diverse backgrounds or multidisciplinary expertise (such as legal or policy experts) in the system's ongoing calibration and review team?

specialists.why: These questions clarify whether legal consultation, human rights alignment, and formal accountability roles under the AI Use Policy have been established, which are not detailed in the current outline.

items.text: Has the project team consulted with agency legal advisors or human rights experts regarding the data processed by the Tripwire pipeline?
options: Yes, formal advice has been obtained
No consultation has occurred or is planned

items.text: Have an Accountable Official and an Accountable Use Case Owner been formally designated for the Tripwire pipeline in accordance with the Policy for the responsible use of AI in government?
options: Only the Accountable Official has been designated
Only the Accountable Use Case Owner has been designated
No, but designation is in progress

items.text: Are there any agency-specific legislative provisions or regulatory instruments that govern the data sources (SQL databases and CSVs) monitored by Tripwire?
options: No, only general administrative and privacy laws apply

specialists.why: Clarification on data contents and records management planning is required to ensure compliance with privacy, Indigenous data governance, and archival standards.

items.text: Does the structured data ingested from SQL databases and CSVs contain any personal information, demographic fields, or data relating to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people?
Has a Business System Assessment Framework (BSAF) review or formal records management plan been scheduled to ensure the SQLite database and logs comply with the Archives Act 1983?

specialists.why: To ensure operational readiness and precise calibration of the pipeline during the pilot phase.

items.text: What specific training or onboarding program, if any, will be provided to data operators to familiarise them with the bi-encoder/cross-encoder architectures and runbooks prior to the observation mode?
Will the 4-8 week observation mode use historical production data, or will it run in parallel with the live manual reconciliation process?
